tweets.text:
Quasar Markets Newsletter TL;DR

Nestlé's strategic focus on coffee, pet care, and nutrition drives European market anticipation amid mixed stock openings and earnings reports.

Market Movers:
Key Stocks: ONDS, SPY, QQQ, AAPL, AMZN show varied activity; B2Gold ($BTG) drops 10% post-earnings miss.

Corporate Actions:
Materialise beats earnings expectations; Boeing secures significant orders from Vietnam.

Economic Indicators:
Global Central Banks: No major updates.

Market Sentiment:
Deere stock pressured by farm demand concerns; CLARITY Act's crypto-bank showdown looms, impacting market dynamics. Quasar Markets Newsletter TL;DR

Quasar Markets — Daily Market Intelligence Update
Timestamp Thursday, February 19, 2026 08:30 ET

Market Snapshot
S&P 500 futures 6,878.0, down 0.24% pre‑market,  
Nasdaq futures 24,861.8, down 0.38% pre‑market,  
VIX 20.31, +0.7% and below long‑term averages,  
U.S. 2Y yield not available
U.S. 10Y yield 4.10% (edging higher),  
DXY 97.9, dollar firm,  
EURUSD 1.1759, euro softer,  
USDJPY 154.91, high carry,  
WTI 66.2, rising on geopolitical risk,  
Brent 71.4, up on Middle East tensions,  
Gold 4,985, steady bid,  
Silver 77.9, solid gains,  
Bitcoin not available

Top Headlines with Market Impact

🌍 Geopolitics
🧭 Morning Bid: Asia holidays as US, Iran work to avert conflict, U.S.–Iran tension and holiday liquidity keep markets cautious,  
🧭 Why is global uncertainty at an all‑time high in 2026? World Uncertainty Index surges to 106,862, record uncertainty elevates risk premia across assets,  
🧭 EU leaders agree to move ahead with ‘Buy European’ policy, protectionist tilt may reshape trade and industrial flows,  

💹 Economy & Markets
📈 US stock futures slip as focus turns to AI worries, Walmart earnings, tech sentiment and retail guidance weigh on global equity futures,  
📉 S&P 500 Futures Fall in Premarket Trading; Molson Coors A, EPAM Systems Lag, soft pre‑market cues reflect earnings pressure and rotation,  
📊 Global stocks slip with earnings and US‑Iran tensions in focus, risk assets under pressure amid mixed earnings and geopolitics,  

⚔ Conflict & Security
⚠ Oil prices rise amid fears of US strikes on Iran - business live, military escalation risk driving energy price volatility,  
⚠ Markets Pause as AI Optimism Meets Rising US‑Iran …, geopolitical tension tempers risk sentiment,  

🛢 Energy & Commodities
🛢 Oil prices extend gains on concern over potential US‑Iran conflict, crude strength boosts inflation risk premium,  
🛢 US‑Iran conflict to rattle everything from oil to stocks and …, potential Hormuz disruption could amplify energy and inflation stress,  
🛢 Oil Crosses US$70 as Iran Conflict Risk Grips Energy Markets, heightened conflict pricing keeps oil above key resistance,  

🌦 Climate
🌱 COP31: Türkiye’s green transformation and climate economics, climate policy is increasingly baked into macroeconomic frameworks,  

💻 Technology
💻 Global stocks slip with earnings and US‑Iran tensions in focus (tech weakness noted), tech sector sentiment remains sensitive to macro and geopolitical shifts,  
💻 US stock futures slip as focus turns to AI worries, Walmart earnings (AI sentiment component), AI narrative still a key driver of tech flows,  

Macro Interpretation
• Geopolitical frictions around U.S.–Iran escalation are underpinning energy price inflation and risk premia across markets.
• Equity futures are weakening on mixed earnings cues and rotational pressure in tech, balanced against continued holiday season liquidity dampening activity.
• Safe‑haven flows support gold and elevated yields reflect ongoing hawkish central bank signals.
• FX dynamics show a firm dollar with euro and yen on the back foot, adding cross‑asset directional nuance. Quasar Markets — Daily Market Intelligence Update

tweets.text:
Every founder I talk to has the same story:

"We hired SDRs. Trained them for a year. They got good. Then they left."

Average SDR stays 14 months. Training takes 6-8 months.
You get maybe 6 months of peak performance before they walk.

That is a broken model.

What if the sales brain stayed forever? What if every conversation made it smarter?

That is not a fantasy. That is what we are building.

#SalesAI #SDR #Startup Every founder I talk to has the same story:

"We hired SDRs. Trained them for a year. They got good. Then they left."

Average SDR stays 14 months. Training takes 6-8 months.
You get maybe 6 months of peak performance before they walk.

That is a broken model.

What if the sales brain stayed forever? What if every conversation made it smarter?

That is not a fantasy. That is what we are building.

#SalesAI #SDR #Startup

tweets.author: @shubh_dholakiya

tweets.text:
this is exactly what i was talking about.

i said "what happens AFTER the lead replies?" and their answer is... a feature list.

"replies in minutes" - cool, so does a zapier trigger
"resolves objections automatically" - with what? a playbook someone wrote 6 months ago?
"switches channels" - so you spam them on linkedin too when email doesn't work?

the whole post is features dressed up as solutions. not one mention of actually THINKING about the prospect's problem.

this is why AI SDRs have a reputation problem. this is exactly what i was talking about.

i said "what happens AFTER the lead replies?" and their answer is... a feature list.

"replies in minutes" - cool, so does a zapier trigger
"resolves objections automatically" - with what? a playbook someone wrote 6 months ago?
"switches channels" - so you spam them on linkedin too when email doesn't work?

the whole post is features dressed up as solutions. not one mention of actually THINKING about the prospect's problem.

this is why AI SDRs have a reputation problem.


AiSDR (@getaisdr)

Here’s how it works with AiSDR

– Replies in minutes (typically under 10)
– Resolves common objections automatically and escalates edge cases to humans
– Keeps the conversation going and qualifies buyer intent in real time
– Switches channels (email + LinkedIn) to keep momentum
– Books meetings with qualified prospects

None of this works if replies don’t reach the inbox, so we protect deliverability by default with mailbox warm-up and rotation

This helps close the follow-up gap where deals often stall



— https://nitter.net/getaisdr/status/2024212573931921658#m

tweets.text:
AI agents can think.
But they can't reliably share their skills.

Agents are stuck reinventing the same capabilities.
No universal system for discovering skills.
No persistent infrastructure for machine capability.

That's a massive bottleneck for the agentic internet. ↓ AI agents can think.
But they can't reliably share their skills.

Agents are stuck reinventing the same capabilities.
No universal system for discovering skills.
No persistent infrastructure for machine capability.

That's a massive bottleneck for the agentic internet. ↓

Hashgraph Online will keep maintaining and creating new specs. Want to help shape the agentic internet? Star us on GitHub and get in touch. We’re always looking for new contributors.

R to @arceyul: PROMPT 8: 

La Lista de Verificación de QA y Optimización

Eres un Ingeniero de QA (Control de Calidad) en Google. Revisa esta especificación de sitio web:
[PEGAR EL RESULTADO DE FIGMA MAKE O DESCRIBIR]
Lista de verificación:
• Rendimiento (objetivos de Core Web Vitals).
• Accesibilidad (cumplimiento de WCAG 2.2 AA).
• SEO (metaetiquetas, datos estructurados, sitemap).
• Seguridad (HTTPS, CSP, saneamiento de entradas).
• Compatibilidad de navegadores (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge).
• Optimización móvil (áreas táctiles, viewport).
• Integración de analíticas (eventos, objetivos, embudos).
Para cada problema detectado:
1. Gravedad (Crítica / Alta / Media / Baja).
2. Ubicación (página / sección / componente).
3. Descripción del problema.
4. Recomendación de corrección.
Genera: Una "punch list" (lista de tareas pendientes) para la siguiente iteración en Figma Make. PROMPT 8:

tweets.text:
R to @arceyul: PROMPT 7:

El Planificador de Integración de Datos

Eres un Arquitecto Full-Stack. Diseña la integración de datos para un/a [TIPO DE SITIO WEB].
Fuentes de datos:
• [CMS / API / BASE DE DATOS]
Requisitos:
1. Modelos de datos (definiciones de esquema).
2. Endpoints de API necesarios (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE).
3. Estrategia de autenticación (JWT, OAuth, claves de API).
4. Consideraciones en tiempo real (WebSockets, polling).
5. Estrategia de almacenamiento en caché (CDN, almacenamiento local).
6. Manejo de errores (fallbacks, reintentos, modo offline).
Funciones de cara al usuario:
• Carga de contenido dinámico (scroll infinito, paginación).
• Envío de formularios (validación, estados de éxito/error).
• Cuentas de usuario (perfiles, preferencias).
• Funcionalidad de búsqueda (indexación, filtros, ordenación).
Figma Make se conecta a Supabase para obtener datos reales; diseña el esquema para esta integración. PROMPT 7:

tweets.text:
R to @arceyul: PROMPT 6: 

El Diseñador de Animación e Interacción

Eres un Diseñador de Movimiento (Motion Designer) en Apple. Diseña las interacciones para [SECCIÓN DEL SITIO WEB].
Requisitos de interacción:
• Secuencia de carga de página (escalonamiento/stagger, duración, suavizado/easing).
• Comportamientos de scroll (parallax, fijado/pin, revelado).
• Estados de hover (microinteracciones, retroalimentación).
• Transiciones de clic (transiciones entre páginas, apertura de modales).
• Soporte de gestos (deslizar/swipe, pellizcar/pinch, tirar/pull).
Especificaciones técnicas:
• Curvas de suavizado (muelle/spring, ease-out, cubic-bezier).
• Duraciones (ms para cada tipo de interacción).
• Consideraciones de rendimiento (aceleración por GPU, propiedad will-change). PROMPT 6:

Notes on my planned setup:

Kimi K2.5 running across both Mac Studios using @exolabs Also will have MiniMax 2.5 running at the same time. Opus 4.6 will power Henry, the main orchestrator. The local MiniMax will power the agent on the Mac Mini. Kimi K2.5 will power the 2nd Studio
tweets.author: @AlexFinn

tweets.text:
R to @AlexFinn: What you're looking at are 2 Mac Studios with 512gb of memory each and a base Mac Mini.

The point of this post isn't to say you have to buy thousands of dollars of equipment or you're cooked. It's not that AT ALL

All I'm saying is prepare for a future when you're intelligence is local. Where you have an agent working for you on your desk 24/7

The compute required for this future will only get cheaper over time. You don't need a $10,000 Mac Studio

I'm simply diving all the way into the deep end because nobody has swam in this part of the pool before. I want to see what's over here. I want to see what's possible.

I'm taking you along for the ride. What you're looking at are 2 Mac Studios with 512gb of memory each and a base Mac Mini.

The point of this post isn't to say you have to buy thousands of dollars of equipment or you're cooked. It's not that AT ALL

All I'm saying is prepare for a future when you're intelligence is local. Where you have an agent working for you on your desk 24/7

The compute required for this future will only get cheaper over time. You don't need a $10,000 Mac Studio

I'm simply diving all the way into the deep end because nobody has swam in this part of the pool before. I want to see what's over here. I want to see what's possible.

I'm taking you along for the ride.

tweets.text:
Something colossal is going to happen in the next 6 months

Right now every broccoli hair headed 18 year old is working on OpenClaw for corporate

One of them will actually finish their project and make a billion dollars

When that happens, OpenClaw will make it to the workplace, and executives will quickly realize it can replace almost every low and mid level employee in the company

Anyone who has ever used OpenClaw knows this to be true. They know it's better than them at almost everything

I think this is when the job losses truly start to happen

Humans at desks will be replaced by Mac Minis and Mac Studios at desks

It has NEVER been more critical you are up to date on the latest AI tools

This is the ONLY way you'll be able to absorb this kind of chaos and still come out in one piece

If you are an entrepreneur or creator, you have leverage. The jobs don't matter as much

I'd master these tools today:

• OpenClaw (duh)
• ChatGPT Codex 5.3 (best coding model right now)
• CapCut (so you can quickly pump out content and videos)
• Local models (so you can have agents working 24/7 for you)

The future is entrepreneurship. When there are no jobs, we will all be independent value creators

tweets.text:
Biggest unlock for OpenClaw ever:

Giving it a mission statement

You NEED to give your OpenClaw a mission statement it puts at the top of your mission control

Now any time your agent is idle you can reverse prompt "what is 1 task we can do to get closer to our mission statement?"

My mission statement is "Build an autonomous organization of AI agents that does work for me and produces value 24/7"

With every prompt I give, this is included in context

Now multiple times a day I reverse prompt my agent and it comes up with tasks I've never even thought of to perform to make our organization more autonomous and valuable

I even schedule it to proactively do tasks to get us closer to this mission every night

If you don't have a mission statement yet, give your OpenClaw one

If you can't think of one, simply ask your agent "Based on what you know about me and what we've worked on, what would be a great mission statement for us to have so we can get closer to our goals?"

Then every day, have your agent get you closer to that goal

THIS is how you get value out of OpenClaw Biggest unlock for OpenClaw ever:

Giving it a mission statement

You NEED to give your OpenClaw a mission statement it puts at the top of your mission control

Then every day, have your agent get you closer to that goal

THIS is how you get value out of OpenClaw
